tasks.description: Extend `PinnedShotGroup` type in `src/tools/video-editor/types/index.ts` to add `start: number` and `children: { clipId: string; offset: number; duration: number }[]`. Keep existing fields (`clipIds`, `mode`, `videoAssetKey`, `imageClipSnapshot`). Add JSDoc invariant comment: children spatially ordered, `clipIds === children.map(c => c.clipId)`, `children[i].offset + children[i].duration === children[i+1].offset`, `start >= 0`.

Create new `src/tools/video-editor/lib/pinned-group-projection.ts` with pure functions: `projectPinnedShotGroups(config)` (rewrites member clip start/end/track from `group.start + child.offset`), `buildGroupFromChildren({ shotId, trackId, start, children, mode, extras? })` (primitive constructor taking children directly), `buildGroupFromConfigClips({ shotId, trackId, clipIds, config, mode, extras? })` (convenience wrapper that looks up clips in `config.clips`, sorts by start, derives start=max(0,min)), `moveGroup(config, groupKey, nextStart, nextTrackId?)` (clamps start>=0), `resizeGroupEdge(config, groupKey, edge, deltaSeconds)` (uniform-scale children), `replaceGroupChildren(config, groupKey, nextChildren)` (preserves start), `normalizeChildren(children)` (sort/butt-collapse/renumber), `findEnclosingPinnedGroup(config, clipId)`, `categorizeSelection(clipIds, config)`. Every writer ends with normalizeChildren.

Wire projection into commit boundaries. Edit `src/tools/video-editor/lib/timeline-save-utils.ts` `buildDataFromCurrentRegistry`: after `repairConfig(migrateToFlatTracks(config))`, call `projectPinnedShotGroups(migratedConfig)` BEFORE `resolvedConfig` is constructed. Edit `src/tools/video-editor/hooks/useTimelineCommit.ts` `applyEdit` for types `'config'` and `'pinnedShotGroups'`: on entry, run the incoming config/override through `projectPinnedShotGroups` so callers passing whole resolvedConfig (from `useClipEditing.ts` and `useTimelineTrackManagement.ts`) cannot bypass projection. Do NOT project inside `assembleTimelineData`. Grep-verify no other materialization path builds rows from raw config.

Update clone helpers to deep-copy `start` and `children`: `clonePinnedShotGroups` in `src/tools/video-editor/lib/migrate.ts:163-172`, the equivalent helper in `src/tools/video-editor/lib/timeline-data.ts:91-100`, and the per-mutation clone in `src/tools/video-editor/hooks/usePinnedShotGroups.ts:31-39`. Then Grep for ad-hoc `PinnedShotGroup` clones inside `src/tools/video-editor/` and fix any remaining sites.

Add idempotent legacy migration to `repairConfig` in `src/tools/video-editor/lib/migrate.ts`: for any `PinnedShotGroup` missing `start` or `children`, look up each `clipId` in `config.clips[]`, drop missing ids, sort by `clip.start`, set `start = max(0, min(clip.start))`, build `children` with offsets relative to that start. Groups with existing `start`+`children` are no-ops.

Create `src/tools/video-editor/lib/pinned-group-projection.test.ts`: unit tests for projection, `buildGroupFromChildren`, `buildGroupFromConfigClips`, `moveGroup` clamp (t>=0), `resizeGroupEdge` uniform scaling, `replaceGroupChildren` preserves start, legacy migration round-trip, plus a property test asserting cohesion-by-construction after random sequences of group ops.

Add shared interaction-state ref as a LOCAL `useRef<{drag: boolean; resize: boolean}>({drag: false, resize: false})` declared in `src/tools/video-editor/components/TimelineEditor/TimelineEditor.tsx` (where `useClipDrag` at :194, `usePinnedGroupSync` at :479, and `TimelineCanvas` at :663-712 all converge). Pass via props/callbacks — NOT through `useTimelineState.contexts.ts`, to avoid provider/type/test churn (per gate's accepted tradeoff). Then: (a) edit `src/tools/video-editor/hooks/useClipDrag.ts` start/end paths to set `interactionStateRef.current.drag = true` on pointerdown after threshold and `false` on pointerup/cancel; (b) edit `src/tools/video-editor/components/TimelineEditor/TimelineCanvas.tsx` `handleResizePointerDown` (:777-843) and `endResize` (:737-775) to set `.resize = true/false` symmetric with existing `pendingOpsRef.current += 1` / `-= 1`; (c) expose `isInteractionActive = () => ref.current.drag || ref.current.resize` closure used by `usePinnedGroupSync`.

Refactor drag to single `GroupDragEntry`. Edit `src/tools/video-editor/hooks/useClipDrag.ts` (:75, :215-237, :278-281): on pointerdown over a clip in a pinned group, record `GroupDragEntry { groupKey, originStart, originTrackId }`. Delete `expandPinnedGroupDrag` and `deferredPinnedGroupClipIds`. Edit `src/tools/video-editor/lib/multi-drag-utils.ts` `planMultiDragMoves`/`applyMultiDragMoves` (:248, :302) to accept heterogeneous moves `{ kind: 'clip', ... } | { kind: 'group', groupKey, nextStart, nextTrackId }`; group moves call `moveGroup`. Simplify `resolveGroupOverlaps`/`findBestGroupStart` in `src/tools/video-editor/lib/resolve-overlaps.ts` (:81, :216) to take a caller-provided `{start, end}` extent; `findBestGroupStart` remains the t>=0 clamp authority. Audit `updatePinnedShotGroupTrackIdsFromClipTrackMap` (:47-80) and delete if all callers migrate.

Refactor resize to outer-edge only. Edit `src/tools/video-editor/hooks/useClipResize.ts`: if resized clip is inside a pinned group, reject. Add `startGroupEdgeResize(groupKey, edge)` entrypoint that calls `resizeGroupEdge`. Edit `src/tools/video-editor/components/TimelineEditor/TimelineCanvas.tsx` resize handlers + `getShotGroupAdjacent`: delete bespoke paired-`ResizeOverride` logic. Replace `positionedShotGroups` (:446-499) in-array-order contiguity check with a direct read of `group.start` + `last(children).offset + last.duration`. Add group-edge resize handles to the overlay wired to `startGroupEdgeResize`.

Update sync path with interaction-state guard. Edit `usePinnedGroupSync` in `src/tools/video-editor/hooks/usePinnedShotGroups.ts` (:153-404): inside the debounced effect callback, read `isInteractionActive()`. If true, skip this tick and re-arm the debounce timer so sync runs after interaction ends. Apply changes via `replaceGroupChildren(config, groupKey, nextChildren)` built by walking `desiredImages` in order with each image's clip duration; `group.start` preserved. Edit `pinGroup` (:98-112) to delegate to `buildGroupFromConfigClips`. Edit `updatePinnedGroup` (:121-144) to never write raw `clipIds` array. Edit `reconcilePinnedShotGroupsInRows` in `src/tools/video-editor/hooks/useTimelineCommit.ts:108-150+` to operate on `children[]`, preserving `group.start`.

Update `src/tools/video-editor/hooks/useExternalDrop.ts:128-193` shot-drop path to build the group inline from the existing loop. The loop at :148-169 already accumulates `timeOffset += nextEdit.duration`; extend it to accumulate a parallel `children` array: push `{ clipId: nextEdit.clipId, offset: timeOffset, duration: nextEdit.duration }` BEFORE the `timeOffset +=` line so offset is the running cumulative. Replace inline group construction at :176-181 with `buildGroupFromChildren({ shotId: shot.id, trackId: resolvedTarget.trackId, start: Math.max(0, dropPosition.time), children, mode: 'images' })`. Do NOT read `workingData.config.clips` — the loop already has all geometry. Downstream `applyEdit({ type: 'rows', ..., pinnedShotGroupsOverride })` unchanged; commit-boundary projection (T3) normalizes output.

Block clip-level breakage on group members across the broader edit surface. Per the brief, ONLY the shot-label menu Deconstruct/Delete may break a group; moving does NOT break cohesion so movement reroutes. (a) Edit `src/tools/video-editor/hooks/useClipEditing.ts:220-236` (single-clip delete) and :351-386 (split): use `findEnclosingPinnedGroup` from T2 to detect the group case and short-circuit as NO-OP; emit error toast 'Use Delete shot from the shot menu' (CLAUDE.md allows error toasts). Free clips keep existing path. (b) `src/tools/video-editor/hooks/useKeyboardShortcuts.ts:96-112`: the guard inside `useClipEditing` implementations handles both keyboard and menu callers through one path. (c) Edit `src/tools/video-editor/hooks/useTimelineTrackManagement.ts:244-331` `moveSelectedClipsToTrack`: before grouping by row, run `categorizeSelection(clipIds, config)`. For each affected group, plan a single group track-move via `moveGroup(config, groupKey, sameStart, nextTrackId)`. Free clips keep per-clip path.

Verify and preserve shot-group menu semantics in `src/tools/video-editor/components/TimelineEditor/TimelineEditor.tsx:691-697`: Unpin/Deconstruct = `unpinGroup(...)` ONLY (member clips remain at last projected positions); Delete shot = `handleDeleteClips(group.clipIds)` THEN `unpinGroup(...)` (both clips and group removed). Do not change. Edit `useSwitchToFinalVideo` and the switch-to-images counterpart to go through `replaceGroupChildren` preserving `group.start`.

Disable per-clip resize handles on group members. Edit `src/tools/video-editor/components/TimelineEditor/ClipAction.tsx` (:23, :162-240): when `isInPinnedShotGroup`, render no per-clip resize handles. Group handles live on overlay (T9).

Delete now-dead cohesion code after callers migrate: `getShotGroupAdjacent` and in-array-order contiguity check (TimelineCanvas.tsx), `deferredPinnedGroupClipIds`/`expandPinnedGroupDrag` (useClipDrag.ts), `resolveGroupOverlaps` collective-extent calc (resolve-overlaps.ts), interior auto-shift in `useClipResize` for the group-member case (non-group clips keep auto-shift). Audit `src/tools/video-editor/hooks/useShotGroups.ts:26-55` — the 'no live clipIds' filter can trust `children`.

Update existing tests for new shape: `src/tools/video-editor/hooks/useShotGroups.test.ts`, `src/tools/video-editor/lib/serialize.test.ts` (add legacy-migration round-trip), `src/tools/video-editor/lib/multi-drag-utils.test.ts` (assert `moveGroup` called for group entities), `src/tools/video-editor/hooks/useClipDrag.test.tsx` (single `GroupDragEntry`), `src/tools/video-editor/components/TimelineEditor/ClipAction.test.tsx` (no per-clip handles for group members), `src/tools/video-editor/components/TimelineEditor/TimelineCanvas.test.tsx` (`positionedShotGroups` reads `group.start`/`children`; leave pre-existing failing `data-shot-group-kind="pinned"` case alone), `src/tools/video-editor/hooks/__tests__/resolve-overlaps.test.ts` (caller-provided extent API), `src/tools/video-editor/hooks/useExternalDrop.test.tsx` (dropped shots create groups with `start` + spatially-ordered `children` built from loop state), `src/tools/video-editor/hooks/useTimelineTrackManagement.test.ts` (ArrowUp/Down & `moveSelectedClipsToTrack` on group member moves whole group). Extend existing delete/split test files with keyboard Backspace/Delete and `s` no-op cases on group members; do NOT create new files if existing files cover those actions.

Add new tests: `src/tools/video-editor/lib/pinned-group-cohesion.test.ts` — property test (random sequences of `moveGroup`/`resizeGroupEdge`/`replaceGroupChildren` always produce contiguous, spatially sorted children after projection), cross-track group move, negative-`start` clamp. Add commit-boundary integration test: legacy config → `buildDataFromCurrentRegistry` → assert (a) member clip starts in `resolvedConfig.clips` match `group.start + child.offset`, (b) `signature` computed from projected config, (c) `applyEdit({ type: 'config', ... })` with pre-projection config still ends up projected. Add sync-deferral test: while `interactionStateRef.current.resize = true`, calling the `usePinnedGroupSync` debounced callback does NOT mutate the group; after `resize = false`, the next tick applies.

Run validation in order: (1) `npx vitest run src/tools/video-editor/lib/pinned-group-projection.test.ts`; (2) `npx vitest run src/tools/video-editor/lib/pinned-group-cohesion.test.ts`; (3) all updated existing tests incl. `useExternalDrop.test.tsx`, `useTimelineTrackManagement.test.ts`, keyboard-no-op cases; (4) commit-boundary integration test; (5) sync-deferral test; (6) `npx tsc --noEmit`; (7) full `npx vitest run src/tools/video-editor/`. Fix failures by reading errors and correcting code — do not suppress. Pre-existing failing TimelineCanvas `data-shot-group-kind="pinned"` test is unrelated; leave it alone. Then write a short throwaway repro script that constructs a legacy config, runs it through `buildDataFromCurrentRegistry`, attempts a drag/resize/sync sequence via the new primitives, and asserts cohesion holds; run it, confirm, delete it.

Run tests relevant to the changed files to verify correctness and check for regressions — run the full test file/module, not just individual functions. Find and run the project's existing test suite — do NOT create new test files. If any test fails, read the error, fix the code, and re-run until all tests pass.

watch_items: CRITICAL LOCATION DECISION (gate override): the shared `interactionStateRef` is a LOCAL `useRef` in `TimelineEditor.tsx`, passed via props/callbacks to `useClipDrag`, `TimelineCanvas`, and `usePinnedGroupSync`. Do NOT lift it into `useTimelineState.contexts.ts` or the split context surface — that would require updating `useTimelineState.types.ts`, `TimelineEditorContext.tsx`, `VideoEditorProvider.tsx`, and `VideoEditorProvider.test.tsx`, which the gate explicitly rejected as unnecessary scope.

Commit-boundary projection must run in TWO places: `buildDataFromCurrentRegistry` (before `resolvedConfig` is built) AND `useTimelineCommit.applyEdit` for types `'config'` and `'pinnedShotGroups'`. Missing either leaves a bypass.
Keyboard Backspace/Delete and `s` (split) on group members are silent NO-OPs (error toast OK); they must NOT reroute to shot deletion. Only shot-label menu may break a group.
ArrowUp/Down and `moveSelectedClipsToTrack` DO reroute — whole-group move via `moveGroup` — because moving preserves cohesion. Do not confuse with delete/split.
`useExternalDrop` builds `children` inline from the loop's own `timeOffset` and `nextEdit.duration`; do NOT read `workingData.config.clips` (pre-commit clips not yet there).
All three clone helpers (`migrate.ts:163`, `timeline-data.ts:91`, `usePinnedShotGroups.ts:31`) must deep-copy both `start` and `children`. Missing a clone site silently strips fields.
`stableSignature` depends on projected config — projection MUST run before signature computation in `assembleTimelineData`'s caller chain.
Delete shot menu preserves `handleDeleteClips(...)` then `unpinGroup(...)`; Unpin/Deconstruct is `unpinGroup` ONLY. Do not change.
Sync-deferral: re-arm the debounce timer when `isInteractionActive()` is true; don't just skip (would starve the sync).
`findBestGroupStart` remains the t>=0 clamp authority in every drag/resize path; `moveGroup` also clamps.
`children[i].offset` is running cumulative; in `useExternalDrop` push BEFORE `timeOffset +=`.
Pre-existing failing `data-shot-group-kind="pinned"` TimelineCanvas test is unrelated — leave alone.
Scope: video-editor tool only. No DB/edge/migration changes. No unrelated cleanups. Follow CLAUDE.md no-toast-on-success rule.

sense_checks.question: Does `PinnedShotGroup` have both `start: number` and `children: { clipId, offset, duration }[]` with JSDoc invariant comment, and are existing fields preserved?
Does `pinned-group-projection.ts` export BOTH `buildGroupFromChildren` (primitive) AND `buildGroupFromConfigClips` (wrapper), and do all writers end with `normalizeChildren`?

Is `projectPinnedShotGroups` called in `buildDataFromCurrentRegistry` BEFORE `resolvedConfig` is built AND in `applyEdit` for types `'config'` and `'pinnedShotGroups'`? Any other materialization paths?
Do all three clone helpers deep-copy `start` AND `children`? Any ad-hoc clones elsewhere that were missed?
Is the legacy migration idempotent, does it drop missing clipIds, and does it clamp start>=0?
Does the property test actually exercise random sequences and assert cohesion-by-construction?

Is the `interactionStateRef` declared as a LOCAL `useRef` in `TimelineEditor.tsx` (NOT lifted into context)? Are drag writes symmetric with pointer threshold and resize writes symmetric with `pendingOpsRef`?
Is `expandPinnedGroupDrag` fully removed? Does `planMultiDragMoves` accept heterogeneous moves? Is `findBestGroupStart` still the t>=0 clamp?
Are interior resizes rejected, per-clip resize on group members removed, and `positionedShotGroups` now reading `group.start`/`children` instead of the in-array contiguity check?
Does `usePinnedGroupSync` re-arm the debounce (not just skip) when `isInteractionActive()` is true? Does `pinGroup` delegate to `buildGroupFromConfigClips`?
Does `useExternalDrop` build `children` inline from its own loop state, NOT from `workingData.config.clips`? Is the push BEFORE `timeOffset +=`?
Are keyboard Delete/split on group members silent NO-OPs (not rerouted)? Do ArrowUp/Down and `moveSelectedClipsToTrack` reroute to whole-group moves via `moveGroup`?

Does Delete shot still call `handleDeleteClips` THEN `unpinGroup`, and Unpin/Deconstruct still call only `unpinGroup`? Do final-video/image switches go through `replaceGroupChildren` preserving `group.start`?
Are per-clip resize handles no longer rendered on group members in `ClipAction.tsx`?
Is all listed dead code removed AND no caller left referencing deleted symbols?
Do all updated tests compile and pass, and are the keyboard no-op cases added to existing test files rather than new ones?

Does the commit-boundary integration test assert the `signature` reflects projection AND that a pre-projection config via `applyEdit` still ends up projected? Does the sync-deferral test prove re-arm (not just skip)?
Did tsc pass, the full video-editor vitest suite pass (minus pre-existing failure), and did the throwaway repro script confirm cohesion holds under drag/resize/sync? Was the script deleted?
Did the verification tests pass? Were any regressions found and fixed?

meta_commentary: Execution order: Phase 1 (T1→T2→T4,T5→T3→T6) builds the foundation with projection and migration tested. T7 (local useRef in TimelineEditor.tsx, NOT context — this is the gate's explicit override) precedes T8/T9/T10 which depend on it. Phase 2 completes the write-path refactor: T8 (drag) → T9 (resize) → T10 (sync) → T11 (external drop) → T12 (edit surface) → T13 (menu verify). Phase 3 (T14,T15) cleans up UI and dead code. Phase 4 (T16,T17,T18) validates. Key judgment calls: (1) The interaction-state ref placement is the single most important execution decision — the plan text says `useTimelineState.contexts.ts` but the gate overrides this and mandates a local `useRef` in `TimelineEditor.tsx` passed via props. Follow the gate. (2) `useExternalDrop` must build children from loop state, not from `config.clips`, because freshly-dropped clips don't exist in config until after commit. Push `children` entries BEFORE incrementing `timeOffset` so offsets are the running cumulative. (3) Keyboard delete/split on group members are silent no-ops with an error toast — they do NOT reroute to shot-deletion semantics. ArrowUp/Down DO reroute because moving preserves cohesion. (4) Commit-boundary projection lives in two places (`buildDataFromCurrentRegistry` AND `applyEdit` for `'config'`/`'pinnedShotGroups'`); missing either leaves a bypass that will break `signature` consistency. (5) The pre-existing `data-shot-group-kind="pinned"` TimelineCanvas test failure is unrelated per the brief — leave it alone. (6) Scope is strictly video-editor tool; no DB/edge/migration changes; no unrelated cleanups.

plan_steps_covered.plan_step_summary: Step 1: Extend PinnedShotGroup type with start + children
Step 2: Add pinned-group-projection.ts module with projection + group ops helpers
Step 3: Wire projection into buildDataFromCurrentRegistry AND applyEdit commit boundary
Step 4: Update every clone helper to deep-copy start+children
Step 5: Load-time migration for legacy configs in repairConfig
Step 6: Unit tests for projection + group ops + migration
Step 7: Lift interaction state (per gate override: LOCAL useRef in TimelineEditor.tsx, not context)
Step 8: Drag refactor to single GroupDragEntry
Step 9: Resize outer-edge only via resizeGroupEdge
Step 10: Sync via replaceGroupChildren + interaction guard
Step 11: useExternalDrop builds group inline from loop state
Step 12: Block clip-level breakage on group members across broader edit surface
Step 13: Preserve shot-group context-menu semantics + mode switch via replaceGroupChildren
Step 14: Disable per-clip resize handles on group members
Step 15: Selection model unchanged (no code task — verified via T12 cohesion guards)
Step 17: Update existing tests + add new cohesion/commit-boundary/sync-deferral tests
Step 19: Manual smoke (info) — covered by the throwaway repro script in T18

validation.completeness_notes: All 19 plan steps mapped. Step 15 (selection model unchanged) is a no-code decision enforced by T12's write-path guards, so it maps to T12. Step 19 (manual smoke, info-priority) is covered by the throwaway repro script in T18. The gate's execution-time override for T7 (local useRef in TimelineEditor.tsx instead of useTimelineState.contexts.ts) is captured in the task description and watch_items — the executor must follow the gate over the plan text here.
